axis: fix builtin file_size + file_exists semantics in runtime.c
trigger: ω-stdlib-1 discovered builtin reads entire file + returns -1 on missing (shadowed portable_fs)

before.file_size_impl: fopen(p,'rb') + fseeko(SEEK_END) + ftello — opens FD unnecessarily, returns -1 on missing/error, behaviour on directory is platform-dependent (BSD fopen on dir returns FD, fseek/ftell may give garbage)
before.file_exists_impl: access(p, F_OK) == 0 — true for ANY entry (directory, symlink-to-dir, socket, FIFO, char/block device), not just regular files
before.interp_dispatch_note: self/hexa_full.hexa:10606 (`file_size` interp dispatch) ALSO bypasses builtin and does `len(read_file(p))` — that's the multi-GB-fatal path; left for a follow-on cycle since constraint limits this session to runtime.c only
bugs: B1: rt_file_size returns -1 on missing — mismatches portable_fs.hexa (pfs_file_size returns 0)
B2: rt_file_size opens FD just for size — wasteful + platform-fragile on directories
B3: rt_file_exists uses access(F_OK) so it returns true for directories, contradicting pfs_file_exists which is `test -f` (regular files only)
B4: hexa_full.hexa interp dispatch for `file_size` reads whole file (out of scope for this cycle's edit-only-runtime.c constraint)

after.file_size_impl: stat(p,&st); if fail → 0; if !S_ISREG → 0; else (int64_t)st.st_size — pure metadata, O(1), missing→0, dir→0
after.file_exists_impl: stat(p,&st); if fail → false; else S_ISREG(st.st_mode) — only true for regular files, missing/dir/socket → false

files_modified.summary: Added <sys/stat.h>; rewrote rt_file_exists (lines ~3467-3482) to stat()+S_ISREG; rewrote rt_file_size (lines ~3656-3677) to stat()-only metadata read; preserved rt_ ABI signatures, no consumer changes.

missing_path.parity: MATCH (was MISMATCH before fix: builtin returned -1, pfs returned 0)

directory_path.parity: MATCH (was MISMATCH before fix: builtin file_exists returned true via access(F_OK), pfs returned false via test -f)

large_file_o1_probe.note: 1.55 GB file — confirmed O(1) latency. Pre-fix interp dispatch (hexa_full.hexa) would have allocated 1.55 GB into RAM via read_file(); old runtime.c fopen+fseek path would at minimum have opened an FD.

host_rebuild.result: OK -> /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp.real (2,763,264 bytes); shim /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp -> hexa_interp.real intact (per ω-toolchain-1)
host_rebuild.smoke: build_interp internal smoke OK; selftest .hexa script subsequently PASSED via host interp

parity_with_portable_fs: MATCH on all three tested cases (small file, missing path, directory). Builtin and pfs_ now return identical values for size (0/n) and existence (true/false) given the same input class. The pfs_* layer is therefore no longer load-bearing for these two functions — kept as legacy/optional aliases per the constraint to leave consumers unchanged this cycle.
next_step_optional: (A) deprecate pfs_* aliases — portable_fs.hexa could re-export the builtins as no-op wrappers (or callers migrate back to bare file_size/file_exists). (B) fix the hexa_full.hexa:10606 interp `file_size` dispatch which still reads the whole file via read_file(); replace with a direct call to the new rt_file_size (would close B4). Both deferred to user.
rollback: git checkout /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/self/runtime.c && cd /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang && HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 ./hexa tool/build_interp.hexa  # restores fopen+fseek file_size and access(F_OK) file_exists. Pre-edit binary also preserved at build/hexa_interp.bak.omega-toolchain-1_20260426 for fast revert.
